The results are shown in Figure 1 as a phylogenetic tree representation. All the human 2019‐nCoV appear very similar to each other, despite the different locations of sampling. Bat coronaviridae appears to be the closet homologs. Two specific specimens gathered in 2013 and 2015 in China from the bat species Rhinolophus affinis and Rhinolophus sinicus appear to be located between the BCoV and the human 2019‐nCoV groups, supporting the notion of a zoonotic transfer from bats to humans.4 Human SARS sequences group with BCoV sequences more distantly related to 2019‐nCoV genomes. Finally, MERS genomes are the most genetically distinct amongst the other sequences.

email template:

Dear _____,

I am writing to concern your recent email containing misleading and incorrect information for your students about the COVID-19 pneumonia aka "Wuhan Acute Respiratory Syndromes" (or simply WARS).

First of all, since WARS caused by "Wuhan conoravirus" are commonly recognized by Hong Kong people, there is no reason to forbid your students using these common terms. For easier communication with Hong Kongers, I will use these terms in the following content.

Regarding to the origin of the Wuhan Conoravirus, a recent report from a group of Chinese scientists does claim that the virus may not be originated from China after examining genomic profiles of several virus samples across the globe. While this piece of information is widely spread online, this study is neither published with a peer-review process nor further supported by any additional evidence or replication study. From a scientific perspective, this kind of conspiracies is as the same as claiming Wuhan Conoravirus is man-made by the Wuhan P4 laboratory or Chinese authorities while I believe that a majority of your students may choose to believe the latter.

Many WARS patients showed "ground glass opacity" in their lung scans, suggesting the virus will attack the respiratory system of their hosts. This mechanism is similar to what SARS and MERS do. On this ground, the International Committee on Taxonomy of Viruse names the Wuhan Conoravirus as the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-COV-2). What causes death and what symptoms they have will not change the fact that the affected are the WARS patients if the test of the virus showed positive results. Therefore, using pneumonia in students' presentation correctly describes the current situation (not mentioning that WHO does use COVID-19 pneumonia as the official name of WARS).

Your email contains incorrect and misleading information. I will not judge your professionality as a teacher. However, from my perspective, I do not see any mistake your students made and any correction needed by them. I have few sincere suggestions for you instead. First, fact-check before you teach your students. Only confirmations from multiple sources can increase your confidence interval in believing
the likelihood of truth. Second, be respect. Students can freely express their opinions and their will in their report. You can rate them a bad grade, it is your right but it will not take away your students' right of free speech. Third, be understanding, a good educator is always a good listener. Listen what your students are concerning about not what you care. No one really cares about you but we Hong Kongers care about the education quality of our next generation. We are happy to hear your public responses about this reply.

Thank you.

Best regards,
An ordinary Hong Konger

email template:

Dear _____,

I am writing to concern your recent email containing misleading and incorrect information for your students about the COVID-19 pneumonia aka "Wuhan Acute Respiratory Syndromes" (or simply WARS).

First of all, since WARS caused by "Wuhan conoravirus" are commonly recognized by Hong Kong people, there is no reason to forbid your students using these common terms. For easier communication with Hong Kongers, I will use these terms in the following content.

Regarding to the origin of the Wuhan Conoravirus, a recent report from a group of Chinese scientists does claim that the virus may not be originated from China after examining genomic profiles of several virus samples across the globe. While this piece of information is widely spread online, this study is neither published with a peer-review process nor further supported by any additional evidence or replication study. From a scientific perspective, this kind of conspiracies is as the same as claiming Wuhan Conoravirus is man-made by the Wuhan P4 laboratory or Chinese authorities while I believe that a majority of your students may choose to believe the latter.

Many WARS patients showed "ground glass opacity" in their lung scans, suggesting the virus will attack the respiratory system of their hosts. This mechanism is similar to what SARS and MERS do. On this ground, the International Committee on Taxonomy of Viruse names the Wuhan Conoravirus as the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-COV-2). What causes death and what symptoms they have will not change the fact that the affected are the WARS patients if the test of the virus showed positive results. Therefore, using pneumonia in students' presentation correctly describes the current situation (not mentioning that WHO does use COVID-19 pneumonia as the official name of WARS).

Your email contains incorrect and misleading information. I will not judge your professionality as a teacher. However, from my perspective, I do not see any mistake your students made and any correction needed by them. I have few sincere suggestions for you instead. First, fact-check before you teach your students. Only confirmations from multiple sources can increase your confidence interval in believing
the likelihood of truth. Second, be respect. Students can freely express their opinions and their will in their report. You can rate them a bad grade, it is your right but it will not take away your students' right of free speech. Third, be understanding, a good educator is always a good listener. Listen what your students are concerning about not what you care. No one really cares about you but we Hong Kongers care about the education quality of our next generation. We are happy to hear your public responses about this reply.

Thank you.

Best regards,
An ordinary Hong Konger

From 沈郁輝回應美國源頭論
真正醫學家朋友回應



昨天分享了深藍群組關於「武漢肺炎源自美國論」的「科學」論述，為正視聽，請了一位醫學教授朋友點評：

//回應藍絲文章，

1) 這文章的分類方法，來自一篇未經同行評審的論文，但論文裡沒有結論說新冠狀病毒來自美國

2) 入面的ABCDE分類，是以全球已公開的96株新冠狀病毒的相似度来分，例如:1-20之間比其他似一些，佢便叫這20個做A, 21-40之間似比其他似一些便叫B，吾係以你爺爺奶奶來分, ABCDE之間是没有從屬關係

3) 那些H什麼，只是在ABCDE下再细分，大家可以不理，網名來的

4) 現時只有中國有齊ABCDE五張閃卡，其他地方只有部分

5) 我在冠狀病毒領域研究多年，沒聽過美國一早有ABCDE，可能我層次太低

6) 全世界都知道，許多中國有錢的人也在美國置產，子女也都在美國讀書，不知道跟傳染的Logic在那裡....

7) 伊朗已知有本地人傳人

8) 如果以新冠病毒所有公開的病株基因、加出現時間畫進化樹，最早的病毒都係喺中國的標本，再散出去其他地方

9) 到現時為止，跟舊SARS 同新SARS最近的兩株蝙蝠病毒，都是在中國找到的

10) 最近大家熱話在穿山甲找到的冠状病毒（有人係馬來西亞找到，所以話新SARS源頭唔係中國），佢只係得一小部份好似，成條基因對比的話，同在中國的蝙蝠找到的遠幾條街，科學意義上，只說明它們只是親戚， 之前出來開記招話喺穿山甲找到新SARS來源的華南農大，已經公開道歉了

11) 我難得有一晚不用做lab可以睇曼聯，唔該唔好發啲垃圾出來阻住我睇波//
